findings.summary: Media identity semantics still vary across generation, shot, and timeline boundaries
evidence: Generation and shot flows still coordinate with multiple ID forms (`id`, `generation_id`, shot-entry IDs) that require adapters across neighboring modules.
Review queue history repeatedly flags callback and identity-shape drift in add-to-shot and timeline-adjacent surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one canonical media identity type at the shared boundary and add narrow adapters only at persistence/API edges, then migrate module signatures to that type incrementally.
findings.reasoning: A stable identity contract removes adapter churn and reduces integration defects across gallery, timeline, and shot-management seams.

findings.summary: Legacy travel/segment migration markers remain on active code paths
evidence: Migration signals still show concentrated deprecated markers in travel-between-images and segment settings pathways.
Compatibility layers remain in active runtime paths instead of being fully isolated at ingress/egress boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate migration behavior behind a single compatibility adapter and retire duplicated legacy branches after converting active callers in the travel + segment settings flow.
findings.reasoning: Reducing migration residue lowers branching complexity and prevents drift between old and new task/settings shapes.
